lyrics:
Hot in the valley
Gotta lose this city
Gonna find a place to soothe my soul
I could take the freeway
But there's just no way
It's gonna take me where I need to go

I'm flying two tree road
I'm flying two tree road
No matter where I go
I'm flying two tree road

There's too much friction
For so little action
My compass spins - it really doesn't know
I need satisfaction
Not indecision
Questions cloud the runway, but I just go

I'm flying two tree road
I'm flying two tree road
No matter where I go
I'm flying two tree road

Just keep on flying

It's not about arriving
It's about the flying
It's about the moments left to chance
The road's dividing
There's no more hiding
Every turn makes all the difference

I'm flying two tree road
I'm flying two tree road
No matter where I go
I'm flying two tree road

I'm flying two tree road
I'm flying two tree road
No matter where I go
I'm flying two tree road
persona:
## PCBender

Rugged, wise, and deeply entrenched in the art of music-making, PCBender is more than just a musician—he’s a sonic craftsman. A guitar-slinging songwriter, producer, and storyteller, he embodies the soul of analog warmth, weaving together rich textures of classic rock, blues, and experimental soundscapes with a modern edge.

With a presence that carries the mystique of a seasoned explorer, PCBender is the Indiana Jones of music production—digging deep into the essence of sound, unearthing raw emotion, and shaping it into songs that resonate beyond time. His music is a fusion of grit and refinement, where worn-in guitar tones meet polished production, and every note feels like it was carved from experience.

A producer at heart, he doesn’t just create songs—he builds them. Each track is an expedition through layers of melody, distortion, and lyrical depth, reflecting both his technical expertise and his philosopher’s approach to songwriting. Whether delivering anthemic riffs, cinematic storytelling, or stripped-down acoustic wisdom, PCBender brings an authenticity that makes listeners feel like they’re sitting in the studio with him, watching music come to life.

As a key figure in Maricopa Records, PCBender bridges the gap between analog and AI-driven creativity, proving that the heart of a song is never about how it’s made, but why.

For those who seek music with depth, texture, and soul—PCBender is your guide.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/0aivrU155laeOcIoT4AhPo?si=pYdQp1J_S4y0vSFA4itgUg)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/pcbender/1793178641)[](https://music.youtube.com/channel/UCvNXkiIHQJM-uY4MT3gmKDw)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/PCBender.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track projects an optimistic and anthemic atmosphere, built on a texture of clean and overdriven guitars that evoke a sense of freedom and escape. The song maintains a driving, high-energy feel, starting with a propulsive verse that explodes into a powerful, wide-open chorus. The production is polished and spacious, with a wide stereo image where all elements sit cleanly together, skillfully contrasting sparser verses with a dense, layered chorus to maximize dynamic impact. The defining feature is the huge, memorable chorus hook, "I'm flying to three row road," which is further elevated by post-chorus vocalizations that cement the track's uplifting, singalong quality.

review.review_text: At 152 BPM in a bright D major, 'Two Tree Road' is built for wide-open spaces, and PCBender earns every bit of that feeling. The chorus hook lands with real force, and the line 'It's not about arriving, it's about the flying' does the lyrical work cleanly, giving the song its thesis without overreaching. The production plays the contrast smartly, letting the verses breathe before the guitars stack up and the track opens into something genuinely singalong-worthy. A highlight.
anchors_used: lyric quote: 'It's not about arriving, it's about the flying'
